sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

# Check for changes
for nid in sorted(set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys()))):
    bn = before_nodes.get(nid)
    an = after_nodes.get(nid)
    if nid >= 29:
        continue
    if bn is None:
        print(f"  NEW id={nid}: color={an['color']} pix={an['pixels']} bound={an['boundary']}")
    elif an is None:
        print(f"  REMOVED id={nid}: color={bn['color']} pix={bn['pixels']}")
    elif bn['hash'] != an['hash'] or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels'] or bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
        print(f"  CHANGED id={nid}: color={bn['color']} pix={bn['pixels']}->{an['pixels']}")

if not any(True for nid in set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys())) 
           if nid < 29 and (before_nodes.get(nid) is None or after_nodes.get(nid) is None 
           or (before_nodes.get(nid)['hash'] != after_nodes.get(nid)['hash'])
           or (before_nodes.get(nid)['pixels'] != after_nodes.get(nid)['pixels'])
           or (before_nodes.get(nid)['boundary'] != after_nodes.get(nid)['boundary']))):
    print("  NO GAMEPLAY CHANGES")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
No changes from clicking the dark gray bar either. Let me think about this differently.
So far, the only things that cause gameplay changes are clicking on the U-shapes inside the containers. These toggle/cycle the source objects between different sizes.
Let me think about what the goal could be. Maybe I need to get the source objects to specific sizes simultaneously. Let me check all possible combinations:
- Green box: 14 or 5 pixels
- Yellow bar: 23, 14, or 5 pixels
That gives 6 possible combinations. I've tried several already. Let me think about what combination would be "correct".
Actually, let me look at the red dots and crosses again. The green box red dot is at row 10, and the top-right cross is at rows 9-11, col 52. The yellow bar red dot is at column 10, and the bottom-left cross is at rows 52, cols 9-11.
Wait, I just realized something. The red crosses are at positions (10, 52) and (52, 10). These are symmetric positions. Maybe the goal is to make the red dots also be at symmetric positions?
Currently:
- Green box red dot: (10, 28) when shrunk, (10, 31) when full
- Yellow bar red dot: (28, 10) when shrunk, (31, 10) when medium, (34, 10) when full
When both are shrunk: (10, 28) and (28, 10) - these are symmetric! Row and column are swapped.
When green is full and yellow is medium: (10, 31) and (31, 10) - also symmetric!
When green is full and yellow is full: (10, 31) and (34, 10) - not symmetric.
So maybe the goal is to have the red dots at symmetric positions. Both the (10, 28)/(28, 10) and (10, 31)/(31, 10) combinations are symmetric.
Currently both are shrunk, giving (10, 28) and (28, 10), which is symmetric. But the level isn't complete.
Hmm, maybe I need the other symmetric combination: green full (10, 31) and yellow medium (31, 10).
Let me try that.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state: green=5 (shrunk), yellow=5 (shrunk)
# Red dots: green at (10, 28), yellow at (28, 10)
# Target: green full (10, 31), yellow medium (31, 10) - symmetric at 31

# Green: click left U-shape to toggle from 5 to 14
# Yellow: click top U-shape to cycle from 5 to 14 (need to check direction)

# From the trace:
# Action 4: yellow 23->14 (clicked top U-shape, went from full to medium)
# Action 5: yellow 14->23 (clicked bottom U-shape, went from medium to full)
# Action 7: yellow 23->14 (clicked top U-shape, went from full to medium)
# Action 8: yellow 14->5 (clicked top U-shape, went from medium to shrunk)
# So clicking top U-shape cycles: 23->14->5->23... (downward)
# Clicking bottom U-shape cycles: 14->23 (upward)

# Current: yellow=5
# Need: yellow=14
# Click bottom U-shape: 5->? (let me check if bottom cycles upward)
# From action 5: bottom clicked, 14->23. So bottom cycles upward: 14->23
# But we need 5->14. Let me try clicking bottom U-shape.

action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 39},  # left green U-shape: toggle 5->14
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 24}   # bottom yellow U-shape: cycle 5->?
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
